title: Physical, Service, and Performance Troubleshooting · Packet Forensics
summary: Resolve a multi-symptom campus incident across cabling, interfaces, switching, routing, addressing, wireless, and application performance. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an evidence-backed incident resolution packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.
Interpret CRC, runts, giants, drops, errors, negotiation, and port status trends with time and traffic context.
Diagnose PoE budget or standard problems and transceiver protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, and signal mismatches.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record

debug case — Use a changed debug case for network troubleshooting that tests this competency: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition..
